Empires & Puzzles Holly Review: The Gnome Chef's Hearty Stew

Holly brings a Hearty Stew to the battlefield, mixing healing, minions, and attack buffs into one pot. But with a Slow mana speed and a reliance on tile luck, is this gnome chef serving a Michelin-star meal or just cold leftovers? We taste-test her kit to see if she deserves a spot on your roster.
Holly Review: Slow Cooked to... Perfection?
Holly: The Gnome Chef Who Cooks Low and Slow
Holly is a 4-star Holy hero from the

(Christmas Event), released back in 2022. She serves as the head chef of the gnome brigade, bringing a support kit that tries to do a little bit of everything: healing, summoning, and buffing. Unlike the fast-paced snacks of modern heroes, Holly believes good things take time—specifically,
mana speed time. While she might not be the first pick for a speed-run, she offers a unique tile-scaling mechanic that can theoretically turn her "Hearty Stew" into a banquet of buffs.

Holly’s design is undeniably charming. She’s the quintessential grandma gnome, complete with a checkered headscarf, a wooden ladle that doubles as a weapon (or perhaps a very aggressive tasting spoon), and a pot of stew that looks like it could cure a cold or melt a tank. It’s a warm, cozy aesthetic that screams "comfort food," which is ironic given that waiting for her mana to charge can be a very uncomfortable experience. She fits perfectly with the festive, lighthearted theme of the Christmas event, standing in stark contrast to the grimdark warriors usually populating the roster.
When we look at Holly's stats in the context of the current meta, the "Slow" cooking metaphor starts to feel a bit too real. With a base Power of
, she is significantly behind modern 4-star heroes, who frequently push the

mark straight out of the portal. Her base Defense (716) and HP (1298) are respectable for her era, leaning towards survivability, which she needs desperately to survive long enough to fire. However, compared to recent powerhouses like
(1445 HP), Holly is starting to show her age. She requires Limit Breaking and heavy embleming just to reach the baseline survivability of newer un-leveled heroes.
is a skill with an incredibly high ceiling but a frustratingly low floor. Let's break down the ingredients:

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

bonus buffs the minions. If you run Holly with another Winter hero (like Buddy or Rudolph), her Elf Minions get a nice stat bump (+5% HP/Atk for 2 heroes). This actually makes the minions surprisingly tanky if you have a full family team, but running multiple Slow/Average minion summoners can clog your team and slow down your matches.
Holly does not have innate passives, which is another mark of her age. Modern heroes usually come with "Resist Ailments" or "Mana on Buff" passives that help them keep up.
is a solid, practical choice for a Slow healer. It helps ensure she stays alive to actually serve the stew. While not as flashy as "Regen" or "Revive," it reinforces her role as a support tank/flank.
When released in late 2022, Holly was already met with skepticism. Players compared her unfavorably to
. She didn't define the meta then, and she certainly doesn't now.
The game has moved towards speed and reliability.
(Fast speed, reflects ailments, boosts attack) essentially does Holly's job but better and faster.
(Slow) deals damage and overheals, offering more utility.
hero for developed rosters. Her only real niche is in
formats where her Slow speed is mitigated, allowing her to fire her powerful scaling buffs more often—assuming the board cooperates.
Since Holly's primary job is to survive until she can cast, and her healing/minion HP is based on her own max HP, you want to bulk her up.
Maximizes survivability and minion HP. Ensures she casts at least once.
Lowers tile damage and minion attack damage (though minion damage is negligible anyway).
. There is no reason to put Attack nodes on Holly unless they are unavoidable. She is a support unit, pure and simple.
If the stars align (literally, the yellow tiles), she can provide a massive 44% heal and 54% attack buff.
Provides a meat shield for the whole team, which helps against snipers.
Yellow lacks accessible 4-star attack buffers (aside from Wu Kong/Bertulf/Guardian Jackal's elemental debuff), so she fills a specific hole for Titans if you lack Miki or Gazelle.
The death knell for most 4-star healers in the current fast-paced meta.
You need to match tiles to charge her, but matching tiles reduces her effectiveness.
A 20% heal at Slow speed can lose you the match if the board is dry.
Needs heavy investment to survive modern snipers.
The only place she truly shines. Set to Very Fast, she charges in 6 tiles, meaning you likely still have yellow tiles left on the board to boost her skill.
Her minions can provide a buffer against incidental damage and curses (if they take the hit).
If you lack better attack buffers, she can serve as a budget booster, provided you use items to charge her while keeping yellow tiles on the board.
Maximize the Winter family bonus to make all those minions beefy. In Rush, this team creates a wall of HP that is annoying to chew through.
Holly - Guardian Jackal - Wu Kong - Joon - Vivica
Holly provides the normal Attack Buff, Jackal gives Elemental Defense Down, and Wu Kong (or Ranvir) adds the gambler's damage.
Note: Cast Holly BEFORE matching tiles to maximize her buff.
Any minion destroyer loves seeing Holly. She spends turn after turn summoning elves just for Grimble to eat them and give mana to his whole team.
Modern 5-star minion counters will absolutely erase her contribution.
Since her heal is inconsistent, she might struggle to clear Fiends if the board is bad.
If Holly is your only 4-star healer, use her! A Slow healer is better than no healer. However, do not prioritize her over
. If you pull her early, level her to 3/60 and see if she fits your playstyle before committing hidden blades and orbs.

She is a collector's item. Unless you are extremely competitive in 4-star Rush Tournaments and lack other yellow options, she is likely not worth the Limit Break aethers. Her "Hearty Stew" is best enjoyed as flavor text rather than a tactical advantage.
The community reaction to Holly was lukewarm from the start. As one player famously put it,
"I hope they fix this hero to average at least. Not tremendously useful at slow."
Another player drew a harsh comparison:
"She is a worse version of Vivica as a 4
However, some optimists saw her potential for Titans:
"So happy to finally have a yellow attack booster for titans, I just hope the minions aren’t a problem."
for most veteran players, often overshadowed by the likes of Goldie and the omnipresent Pig.

Holly is a hero with a fun theme and an interesting mechanic that is sadly held back by her mana speed and the contradictory nature of her skill. She asks you to hoard tiles but demands you match them to work. In a game that favors speed and reliability, Holly is a slow Sunday roast in a fast-food world. She’s edible, she’s cozy, but she probably won’t win you the culinary competition.
(unless you really love Christmas or Rush Tournaments).

A comprehensive review of Holly, the 4-star Holy Cleric from the Winter Family. Is her slow-cooked stew worth the wait, or should you order takeout from Goldie? We analyze her stats, tile-scaling skills, and modern viability.
